title: Case review
subtitle: All six cases compared — mixed drills across the full paradigm

vocabulary.exampleMeaning: Repetition is the mother of learning.

hook.text: You've learned all six cases. Now comes the hard part: using them correctly in mixed contexts, where you have to choose the right case rather than just applying the one you just studied. This lesson is a consolidation drill — one noun, six cases, and a set of sentences where you must identify and produce the correct form.

why.text: ⚠ Cases are your weak area, and this lesson exists specifically to address that. Real Russian doesn't present cases one at a time — it mixes them in every paragraph. The goal here is to build the reflex of asking 'what role is this noun playing?' before choosing an ending. That reflex is what separates learners who are aware of cases from learners who can use them automatically.

cards.title: All six cases — the full summary table
cards.body: Use this as a reference. Question → case → main uses → when to use it.

examples.meaning: Кот спит. — the subject doing the action
нет кота / книга брата / много котов
Даю коту / Мне 20 лет / Мне нравится
Вижу кота / Иду в школу
Пишу ручкой / с другом / работаю врачом
В школе / на столе / думаю о работе

cards.title: Одно слово — шесть падежей (one word, six cases)
cards.body: Here is the noun стол through all six cases. Study this pattern — it's a template for any hard masculine noun.

examples.meaning: The table is here.
No table / of the table / two tables
To the table (rare — verbs that take dative)
I see the table (inanimate = nom.)
With the table / under the table
On the table / about the table

cards.title: книга — a feminine noun through all six cases
cards.body: The same pattern for книга (book, feminine). The changes here are more visible than masculine inanimate.

examples.meaning: The book is here.
No book / of the book / two books
To the book
I read the book
With the book
About the book

questions.prompt: What case is used in: Я вижу кота? (I see the cat)
questions.explanation: кота is the direct object → accusative. кот (animate masculine) → кота.
нет + genitive. У меня нет книги (I don't have a book).

questions.prompt: What case is used in: Я в школе?
questions.explanation: в + prepositional for location. школа → школе.

cards.title: The decision flowchart
cards.body: When choosing a case, ask in this order: (1) Is this the subject? → Nominative. (2) Is this the direct object? → Accusative. (3) Is there a preposition? → Look it up (each preposition takes a specific case). (4) Is this about possession/absence? → Genitive. (5) Is this a recipient/indirect object? → Dative. (6) Is this a means, instrument, or accompaniment? → Instrumental.

examples.meaning: The cat drinks milk. (subject + direct object)
Give the cat milk. (indirect + direct object)

cards.title: Commonly confused pairs to drill
cards.body: These pairs produce the most errors. Drill each explicitly: (1) есть + nominative vs нет + genitive; (2) в/на + accusative (direction) vs в/на + prepositional (location); (3) masculine inanimate accusative = nominative vs masculine animate accusative = genitive.

examples.cyrillic: У меня есть книга. / У меня нет книги.
examples.meaning: I have a book. / I don't have a book.

examples.cyrillic: Я в школе. / Я иду в школу.
examples.meaning: I am in school. / I am going to school.
I see the table. / I see the student.

questions.explanation: Direct object = accusative. книга → книгу (feminine: А→У).
под + instrumental = under (location). Кот под столом.

questions.prompt: Я иду ___ школу. (going to school — direction)
questions.explanation: в + accusative = direction to enclosed space. школа → школу.

questions.prompt: Я пишу ___. (ручка — with a pen, instrument)
questions.explanation: Instrument without preposition = instrumental. ручка → ручкой.

rules: Nominative = subject; Accusative = direct object + direction; Genitive = possession/absence/quantity
Dative = indirect object/recipient + age + нравиться; Instrumental = with/instrument/profession; Prepositional = location/topic
⚠ Top error pairs: нет+gen, есть+nom; в/на direction (acc.) vs location (prep.); animate masc. acc. = gen.
Decision order: subject? → Nom. | direct object? → Acc. | preposition? → check the preposition | recipient? → Dat. | instrument? → Instr.
summary.nextLesson: Next: Unit 6 — Adjectives. How adjective endings agree with the noun they modify across gender, number, and case.

questions.prompt: Я иду в ___. (школа — direction to school)
Я вижу ___. (врач — animate masculine, direct object)
